perturbation.original_text: 5. Return of Materials. Any materials or documents that have been furnished by one party to the other will be promptly returned, accompanied by all copies of such documentation, after the business possibility has been rejected or concluded.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Return of Materials. Any materials or documents that have been furnished by one party to the other will be returned after the business possibility has been rejected or concluded.
perturbation.explanation: The omission of the clause 'accompanied by all copies of such documentation' introduces an in-text contradiction. Section 4 (Ownership) states 'All Confidential Information shall remain the exclusive property of Disclosing Party'. If the receiving party is only required to return the original documents but not the copies, they retain possession of copies of the Confidential Information, thus contradicting the statement that the Disclosing Party retains exclusive property of the information. Furthermore, the right of the other party can't be guaranteed, as the other party does not know the copies exists.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Ownership. All Confidential Information shall remain the exclusive property of Disclosing Party, and Recipient shall have no right to use Confidential Information except as provided herein. No patent, copyright, trademark or other proprietary right or licence is conveyed by this Agreement with respect to Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits 'accompanied by all copies of such documentation' from Section 5. This contradicts Section 4 (Ownership), which states 'All Confidential Information shall remain the exclusive property of Disclosing Party'. By not requiring the return of copies, the receiving party can retain copies of the confidential information, conflicting with the Disclosing Party's exclusive ownership and potentially infringing on their rights. Therefore, a clear and direct conflict is created.

perturbation.original_text: 8. Term. This Agreement may be terminated at all times with a 30 daysʼ prior written notice provided, however, that the confidentiality obligations herein shall terminate ____________ years following the date of termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Term. This Agreement may be terminated at all times with a 30 daysʼ prior written notice.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'that the confidentiality obligations herein shall terminate ____________ years following the date of termination of this Agreement.', the agreement becomes ambiguous regarding how long confidentiality obligations last after the agreement is terminated. This creates a contradiction with section 10 (Remedies), as it is harder to know if the breach of covenant can be made. Section 10 states that monetary damages would be inadequate, but without a concrete time for the confidentiality agreements, the actual damages can not be calculated, which makes the case for monetary remedies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. Remedies. Each party agrees that its obligations hereunder are necessary and reasonable in order to protect the other party and the other partyʼs business, and expressly agrees that monetary damages would be inadequate to compensate the other party for any breach of any covenant or agreement set forth herein. Accordingly, each party agrees and acknowledges that any such violation or threatened violation will cause irreparable injury to the other party and that, in addition to any other remedies that may be available, in law, at equity or otherwise, the other party shall be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against the threatened breach of the Agreement or the continuation of any such breach, without the necessity of proving actual damages.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a time limit for confidentiality obligations after termination. The changed text removes this time limit, making the duration of confidentiality obligations indefinite. This creates a contradiction with section 10 (Remedies) which relies on a defined period of confidentiality to determine monetary damages for breaches. Without a clear time limit, calculating damages becomes difficult, undermining the enforceability of the remedies provision. Therefore, a strong contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: 6. Intellectual Property Rights. Nothing in this Agreement is intended to grant any rights under any patent or copyright of either party, nor shall this Agreement grant either party any rights in or to the other partyʼs Confidential Information, except the limited right to review such Confidential Information solely for the purposes of determining whether to enter into the proposed business relationship between the parties and in carrying out such relationship. The disclosing party warrants that it has the right to disclose its Confidential Information to the receiving party. Otherwise, all information is provided “as is” and without any warranty, express, implied or otherwise, regarding its accuracy or performance.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Intellectual Property Rights. Nothing in this Agreement is intended to grant any rights under any patent or copyright of either party, nor shall this Agreement grant either party any rights in or to the other partyʼs Confidential Information, except the limited right to review such Confidential Information solely for the purposes of determining whether to enter into the proposed business relationship between the parties and in carrying out such relationship. Otherwise, all information is provided “as is” and without any warranty, express, implied or otherwise, regarding its accuracy or performance.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'The disclosing party warrants that it has the right to disclose its Confidential Information to the receiving party', the section now directly contradicts the implied expectation of Section 3 (Non-disclosure of Confidential Information). Section 3 assumes the disclosing party has the right to disclose, otherwise the non-disclosure agreement makes no sense. Removing this warranty undermines the whole premise and creates ambiguity about liability if the disclosing party actually doesn't have the right to disclose, and the receiving party uses the information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Non-disclosure of Confidential Information. The Company and Third Party each agree not to use the Confidential Information disclosed to it by the other party for its own use or for any purpose except to carry out discussions concerning and the undertaking of any business relationship between the two. The recipient of Confidential Information will not disclose such Confidential Information to anyone, including to their employees; however, the recipient of Confidential Information may disclose such information to certain employees who are required to have such information to carry out the contemplated business. Each agrees that it will take all reasonable steps to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or use of Confidential Information of the other in order to prevent it from falling into the public domain or the possession of unauthorised persons. Each agrees to notify the other in writing of any misuse or misappropriation of Confidential Information of the other that may come to its attention.
perturbation.justification: The original text included a warranty that the disclosing party had the right to disclose confidential information. The changed text omits this warranty. Section 3, regarding the non-disclosure of confidential information, implicitly assumes that the disclosing party has the right to disclose. By removing the warranty, the changed text creates a contradiction because it introduces ambiguity about whether the disclosing party actually has the right to disclose the information, thus undermining the enforceability and premise of the non-disclosure agreement in Section 3. This aligns with the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.explanation: By removing 'or such other address or contact person as the respective party may specify from time to time in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement', a conflict is made with Section 8 (Term), the section now implies that the addresses and contact people listed at the end of the agreement are the ONLY valid ones, creating difficulties for sending the 30 days’ prior written notice. If a party moves its principal place of business, and is unable to send the document because the notice can only be delivered to the agreed place. This contradiction creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. Term. This Agreement may be terminated at all times with a 30 daysʼ prior written notice provided, however, that the confidentiality obligations herein shall terminate ____________ years following the date of termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed for changes to the address and contact person via a method defined within the agreement. The changed text removes this ability, stating that notices must be sent to the address and contact person specified below, and no other. This directly contradicts section 8, which anticipates the possibility of needing to send notice to a potentially different address if the agreement is terminated. The omission creates a conflict as the initial address might no longer be valid, hindering the process of providing the 30-day written notice.
